name: screen

metadata.name: screen
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# screen

Screen width and so on

## Installation

```
pip install screen
```

## Usage

```
import screen

screen.calc_width(u'测试',0, 2)
```

functions.name: screen.old_str_util.get_width
documentation: Return the screen column width for unicode ordinal o.
ACCESSOR FUNCTIONS

functions.name: screen.old_str_util.decode_one
documentation: Return (ordinal at pos, next position) for UTF-8 encoded text.

functions.name: screen.old_str_util.decode_one_uni
documentation: decode_one implementation for unicode strings

functions.name: screen.old_str_util.decode_one_right
documentation:
Return (ordinal at pos, next position) for UTF-8 encoded text.
pos is assumed to be on the trailing byte of a utf-8 sequence.

functions.name: screen.old_str_util.calc_text_pos
documentation:
Calculate the closest position to the screen column pref_col in text
where start_offs is the offset into text assumed to be screen column 0
and end_offs is the end of the range to search.

text may be unicode or a byte string in the target _byte_encoding

Returns (position, actual_col).

functions.name: screen.old_str_util.calc_width
documentation:
Return the screen column width of text between start_offs and end_offs.

text may be unicode or a byte string in the target _byte_encoding

Some characters are wide (take two columns) and others affect the
previous character (take zero columns).  Use the widths table above
to calculate the screen column width of text[start_offs:end_offs]

functions.name: screen.old_str_util.is_wide_char
documentation:
Test if the character at offs within text is wide.

text may be unicode or a byte string in the target _byte_encoding

functions.name: screen.old_str_util.move_prev_char
documentation: Return the position of the character before end_offs.

functions.name: screen.old_str_util.move_next_char
documentation: Return the position of the character after start_offs.

functions.name: screen.old_str_util.within_double_byte
documentation:
Return whether pos is within a double-byte encoded character.

text -- byte string in question
line_start -- offset of beginning of line (< pos)
pos -- offset in question

Return values:
0 -- not within dbe char, or double_byte_encoding == False
1 -- pos is on the 1st half of a dbe char
2 -- pos is on the 2nd half of a dbe char
